description_long: This object is a cluster of delicate, light pink flowers with yellow and white centers, each petal showing subtle gradients of color and some natural blemishes. The flowers are arranged in a loose, somewhat radial cluster at the end of a green stem, which is adorned with several dark green, ovate leaves. The petals are thin, slightly translucent, and overlap each other, creating a layered effect. The central parts of the flowers display visible stamens with yellow tips, adding to the naturalistic detail. The stem is slender and slightly curved, supporting both the flowers and a few unopened buds. The overall appearance is that of a wild or garden rose, suitable for placement in parks, gardens, or landscaped urban areas.
description: A cluster of light pink flowers with yellow centers and green ovate leaves on a slender stem, featuring layered petals and visible stamens.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main cluster of pink flowers with yellow centers, several leaves, and a visible unopened bud.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the overlapping petals and the arrangement of flowers, with more leaves visible along the stem.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the back of the flower cluster and the curvature of the stem, with leaves extending outward.
description_view_3: Side view with a focus on the unopened bud and the lower leaves, as well as the layered petal structure.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, overlapping in a whorled pattern around the center of each flower, with some layering and slight asymmetry typical of wild rose species.
